# DebtConsolidationApp.com

> A free, AI-powered debt relief assessment tool built by Adam Tijerina — a personal finance expert with 15+ years of experience and 9 years at National Debt Relief. The tool analyzes your specific financial profile (debt amount, income, credit score, number of creditors, home purchase timeline, and priorities) and ranks all five debt relief options — consolidation loan, balance transfer, debt management plan, debt settlement, and bankruptcy — by suitability for your situation. No email required. No sales call. Instant results.

Website: https://debtconsolidationapp.com
Author: Adam Tijerina
Language: en
License: All rights reserved

---

## Core Tool

- [Free Debt Assessment](https://debtconsolidationapp.com/assessment): 8-question AI-powered assessment that analyzes your financial profile and ranks all five debt relief options by suitability. Returns a personalized recommendation with suitability scores, credit impact ratings, estimated timelines, pros/cons, and a plain-language explanation for each option.

- [Results Page](https://debtconsolidationapp.com/results): Displays ranked debt relief recommendations with expandable detail cards, a savings calculator comparing minimum payments vs. settlement, an options comparison table, and an AI chat assistant for follow-up questions.

- [Scenarios Library](https://debtconsolidationapp.com/scenarios): 30 real-world debt scenarios showing how the tool recommends different options for different financial profiles — from first-time homebuyers with existing debt to retirees on fixed income to gig workers with irregular income.

---

## Key Pages

- [Home](https://debtconsolidationapp.com/): Landing page explaining how the tool works, the five debt relief options, and who the tool is for.

- [About Adam Tijerina](https://debtconsolidationapp.com/about): Background on the author — 15+ years in personal finance, 9 years at National Debt Relief, personally settled $43,000 in credit card debt.

- [Blog](https://debtconsolidationapp.com/blog): Educational articles on debt relief, written in Adam's voice and grounded in CFPB, Federal Reserve, BLS, and FTC data.

---

## Blog Posts

- [The FTC Just Shut Down a Debt Relief Scam Targeting Seniors — Here's How to Spot a Fake](https://debtconsolidationapp.com/blog/cfpb-ftc-halts-debt-relief-scam-2025): In July 2025, federal regulators halted an illegal debt relief operation that impersonated real businesses and government agencies to steal money from seniors and veterans. Here's what happened and how to protect yourself.

- [Americans Now Owe $1.28 Trillion on Credit Cards. Here's What That Number Means for You.](https://debtconsolidationapp.com/blog/credit-card-debt-1-28-trillion-2026): The Federal Reserve Bank of New York confirmed that U.S. credit card debt hit $1.28 trillion in late 2025 — a record high. The average cardholder with an unpaid balance owes $7,886. Here's what's driving it and what you can actually do about it.

- [You Just Got Laid Off and You Have Debt. Here's Exactly What to Do in the First 72 Hours.](https://debtconsolidationapp.com/blog/laid-off-debt-what-to-do-first-2026): With U.S. unemployment at 4.2% and payroll growth slowing to just 57,000 jobs in June 2026, more Americans are facing the terrifying combination of job loss and debt. Here's the exact playbook for the first 72 hours.

- [How to Negotiate Your Own Debt Settlement (The Complete Guide for 2026)](https://debtconsolidationapp.com/blog/how-to-negotiate-debt-settlement-yourself-2026): "Can I negotiate my own debt settlement?" is one of the most-searched debt questions on Google. The answer is yes — but only under specific circumstances. Here's the complete guide to DIY debt settlement, including the scripts that actually work.

- [Bankruptcy Filings Rose 11% in 2025 — A 10-Year High. Is It the Right Move for You?](https://debtconsolidationapp.com/blog/bankruptcy-filings-rise-11-percent-2025-what-it-means): The U.S. Courts confirmed that non-business bankruptcy filings rose 11.2% in 2025, reaching a 10-year high. Chapter 11 business filings also hit their highest level in a decade. Here's what's driving the surge and how to know if bankruptcy is the right option for your situation.

- [$1.25 Trillion and Climbing: The Real Story Behind America's Credit Card Debt Crisis](https://debtconsolidationapp.com/blog/us-credit-card-debt-record-2025): Americans are now carrying $1.25 trillion in credit card debt — and the average APR just hit a 30-year high of 20.79%. Here's what the data actually means, why it happened, and what you can do before the numbers get worse.

- [Your Credit Card Debt Is Making You Physically Sick. Here's the Science.](https://debtconsolidationapp.com/blog/credit-card-debt-physical-health-effects): Research published in JAMA, APHA, and the NIH links carrying credit card debt to higher rates of high blood pressure, heart disease, obesity, and skipping medical care. The stress of debt isn't just in your head — it's in your body.

- [Debt Is Destroying Your Sleep, Your Mental Health, and Your Relationships. Here's the Evidence.](https://debtconsolidationapp.com/blog/credit-card-debt-mental-health-sleep-relationships): A growing body of research links credit card debt to clinical anxiety, depression, insomnia, and relationship breakdown. 71% of people with financial hardship report sleep disturbances. Money fights are the #1 predictor of divorce. This is what debt actually costs.

- [At 20% APR, Here's Exactly How Long It Takes to Pay Off Your Credit Card Debt](https://debtconsolidationapp.com/blog/how-long-to-pay-off-credit-card-debt-high-interest): If you're making minimum payments on a $10,000 credit card balance at today's average 20% APR, you'll be in debt for 28 years and pay $16,000 in interest. Here's the math — and the four strategies that actually work.

- [The Uncomfortable Truth About Credit Counseling: It's Designed to Get Creditors Paid in Full](https://debtconsolidationapp.com/blog/credit-counseling-helps-creditors-more-than-you): Nonprofit credit counseling agencies receive funding from the same banks and credit card companies whose debts you're trying to manage. Under a Debt Management Plan, you pay back every dollar you owe — just at a lower interest rate. Here's what that means for you.

- [Minimum Payment Trap — High Interest Debt](https://debtconsolidationapp.com/scenarios/minimum-payment-trap-high-interest-debt)

- [Maxed-Out Cards, No Savings — Settlement](https://debtconsolidationapp.com/scenarios/maxed-out-cards-no-savings-settlement)
- [High-Interest Store Cards + Personal Loans](https://debtconsolidationapp.com/scenarios/high-interest-store-cards-personal-loans-settlement)
- [Layoff + Accumulated Debt — Settlement](https://debtconsolidationapp.com/scenarios/layoff-accumulated-debt-settlement)

- [Self-Employed, Irregular Income, High Debt](https://debtconsolidationapp.com/scenarios/self-employed-irregular-income-high-debt)
